notes: Block-level networked storage; article prescribes centralizing data center storage on SANs as the first step toward ILM
File-level networked storage supplementing SANs; described as affordable for even small enterprises by 2004
High-performance online dynamic storage for OLTP and high-activity DSS; positioned as most expensive tier in four-pool model
Capacity-oriented disks at 25% of FC/SCSI cost for fixed content and compliance data; evolved into modern capacity/cool storage tiers including SATA and cloud object storage
Using disk libraries instead of tape for backup and restore; described as compressing backup times by up to 50%; successor to tape libraries
Cross-application policy-driven storage management; predicted to mature circa 2008-2010; evolved into modern storage tiering and data lifecycle management platforms
Sequential offline storage for disaster recovery; article warns of litigation liability from retained tapes; persists for cold storage and compliance archival
Abstracting physical storage into logical pools; cited as key enabler for economies of scale and rising TB-per-admin ratios
